pages.full_text: Tuesday  We had a lie in + set off to Vezelay by quite complicated main roads. At the carpark, halfway down the hill,
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in postcard/photo of the interior of Vezelay basilica. Text continues onto right page. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. The caption 'Vezelay on Yonne' is written below the photo in blue/black ink.

pages.full_text:
I had to wait while Simon went off to find the €6. We had lots of time to look at the west wall, 2 of them, inner + outer, + all the capitals. At one time 1000 pilgrims were burned when the cathedral caught fire. It was involved with crusades. Richard the [lion]heart visited.

    We started with lunch + I had chocolate waffles gou[?]ffles, for pudding.
*****
    On to Chaumot, near Corbigny run by a very relaxed Dutchman. He bought a station house near here 25 years ago + this campsite 14 years ago. The loos are brilliant – easy to clean – loo, bin, brush off the floor. It's on Canal Nivernais, so we can bike ride tomorrow. It started to rain when I was in the loo + has gone on + on. Sodden cyclists keep arriving...
Wednesday July 29th.

We are on a sort of island with a bridge at one end. There was a railway here once which interchanged with the canal. The next village has "mines" in
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 74 in top-right corner. Text is a continuation from left page about the Vezelay cathedral visit, then moves to a campsite at Chaumot near Corbigny run by a Dutchman on Canal Nivernais. The word after 'waffles' is partially overwritten/corrected — reads 'gouffles' or possibly 'gaufres' (French waffles); marked as [?]ffles. 'Richard the lionheart' — the word 'lion' appears as a combined word 'lionheart'; text reads 'Richard the [lion]heart'. Year 2009 inferred because July 29 fell on a Wednesday in 2009, which fits the volume date range (2003–2019) and the use of euro currency. Text is cut off at bottom of page.
